---
title: "City2Graph · Import urban data"
---

::: {.lead}
Bring urban features into City2Graph for Burjassot: Overture buildings and
places, local GIS files, and GTFS public-transport feeds.
:::

This walkthrough mirrors the [Data Import panel](../../panels/city2graph/data.qmd).

## Before you begin

Open the **City2Graph** tab. To align downloads with an existing street network,
keep an OSMnx network active and use **From OSMnx** as the area method.

## 1. Define the area

In **Data Import**, set **Define Area**. The simplest option is **Place** with
`Burjassot, Valencia, Spain`. Other methods (**From OSMnx**, **Point**,
**Address**, **BBox**, **Polygon**) reuse the OSMnx area fields.

![Defining the Burjassot area in Data Import.](../../../assets/guides/city2graph/01-define-area.png)

## 2. Download Overture features

Toggle the **Overture** layers you need — **building**, **segment**, **place**,
**water**, **land** — and set a **Limit**. Press **Get as Mesh** for footprints,
or **Get as Points** to create point nodes directly.

![Selecting Overture layers and downloading.](../../../assets/guides/city2graph/01-overture.png)

## 3. Convert to centroids

Press **Convert to Centroids** to reduce polygon footprints to one point each —
the node representation that proximity graphs expect.

![Converting building footprints to centroids.](../../../assets/guides/city2graph/01-centroids.png)

## 4. Load a local file

Press **Load File** and pick a GeoPackage, Shapefile or GeoJSON to import your
own zones or features.

![Loading a local GIS file.](../../../assets/guides/city2graph/01-load-file.png)

## 5. Import GTFS

Set the **GTFS Path** (a São Paulo feed ships under `examples/GTFS/`) and press
**Import GTFS**. Then **Visualize GTFS** to draw stops and routes.

![Importing and visualizing a GTFS feed.](../../../assets/guides/city2graph/01-gtfs.png)

## Next steps

With features in hand, continue with
[Build a morphological graph](morphology.qmd) or
[Build proximity graphs](proximity.qmd).
